moments.title: Ice Cube turns 57
moments.blurb: Hip-hop heavyweight and N.W.A founder Ice Cube celebrates his birthday today.

angles.name: It was a good day
angles.copy: Ice Cube is 57 today. And you already know what we’re playing. It was a good day.

angles.name: The catalogue
angles.copy: N.W.A, Friday, a stack of solo hits and a face that launched a thousand memes. Ice Cube is 57 — still cooler than all of us.

moments.title: Ariana Grande is this week’s No.1
moments.blurb: Ariana Grande tops the UK singles chart this week with “hate that i made you love me”.

angles.name: Straight in at No.1
angles.copy: Ariana Grande goes straight to No.1 this week with ‘hate that i made you love me’. On heavy rotation — you’re welcome.

angles.name: Song of the summer?
angles.copy: New week, new No.1. Ariana’s on top — but is it your song of the summer, or has something else got it? Make your case.

moments.title: Sam Fender back in the top 10
moments.blurb: North East favourite Sam Fender is in the top 10 with “Rein Me In”, featuring Olivia Dean.

angles.name: Local hero
angles.copy: Our Sam’s done it again. Sam Fender, ‘Rein Me In’ with Olivia Dean, straight into the top 10 — and the North East could not be prouder.

angles.name: The collab
angles.copy: Sam Fender and Olivia Dean on the same track? Yes please. ‘Rein Me In’ is flying up the chart — have a listen.

moments.title: Love Island, one week in
moments.blurb: Love Island is a week into its summer run on ITV2, six nights a week and dominating group chats.

angles.name: Morning-after chaos
angles.copy: One week of Love Island and we’ve already got a villain, a “type on paper”, and at least one grafting disaster. We’re not okay. We’re not turning it off either.

angles.name: Pick a side
angles.copy: A week in. Who’s your winner, and who needs dumping tonight? No wrong answers — only strong ones.

angles.name: Recoupling soundtrack
angles.copy: Every recoupling deserves a dramatic soundtrack. We’ve got it covered, while you pretend you’re not invested.

moments.title: Air Supply’s Russell Hitchcock is 77
moments.blurb: Russell Hitchcock, the voice of Air Supply, celebrates his birthday today.

angles.name: All out of love
angles.copy: Russell Hitchcock of Air Supply is 77 today. All Out of Love, Making Love Out of Nothing at All — Nation Easy’s bread and butter. Many happy returns.

moments.title: Royal Ascot opens
moments.blurb: Royal Ascot begins Tuesday — five days of racing, hats and high-confidence tips.

angles.name: The sweepstake joke
angles.copy: Royal Ascot. Five days. Horses nobody’s heard of, hats that cost more than the horses, and a sweepstake that’ll end a friendship.

angles.name: Here for the outfits
angles.copy: Be honest — are you here for the racing or the outfits? Royal Ascot’s back, and the hats are already winning.

angles.name: Tip tracker
angles.copy: Royal Ascot, day one. Give us your tip for the feature race and we’ll track whose is best all week.

moments.title: Messi and Argentina back in action
moments.blurb: Holders Argentina face Algeria on Tuesday night — possibly Lionel Messi’s last World Cup.

angles.name: The last dance
angles.copy: Argentina are back tonight, and so is Messi — quite possibly his last World Cup. Whatever shirt you wear, you watch this one.

moments.title: Foreigner & Jefferson Starship in Manchester
moments.blurb: Classic-rock royalty Foreigner and Jefferson Starship play the AO Arena, Manchester this week.

angles.name: Know what love is
angles.copy: Foreigner and Jefferson Starship at Manchester’s AO Arena this week. I Want to Know What Love Is, incoming. Who’s going?

moments.title: England open their World Cup v Croatia
moments.blurb: England begin against Croatia on Wednesday night — a rematch of the 2018 semi-final.

angles.name: The rematch
angles.copy: Last time England got near a final, Croatia ended it. Wednesday night, they’re first up. The rematch, eight years on.

angles.name: Manage expectations
angles.copy: England’s World Cup starts Wednesday. Manage your expectations — then immediately ignore that advice. Croatia, 9pm.

angles.name: Where are you watching
angles.copy: Big screen, beer garden, or behind the sofa? England v Croatia, Wednesday night. Where will you be?

moments.title: Paul McCartney turns 84
moments.blurb: Paul McCartney celebrates his 84th birthday on Thursday.

angles.name: Still going
angles.copy: Paul McCartney is 84 on Thursday and still selling out stadiums. The Beatle who never stopped. We’ll mark it properly.

angles.name: The songbook
angles.copy: Yesterday. Hey Jude. Let It Be. Live and Let Die. Band on the Run. One man wrote half your life’s soundtrack. Happy 84th, Macca.

angles.name: Request
angles.copy: It’s Macca’s birthday Thursday. Beatles, Wings or solo — tell us the one we have to play.

moments.title: Alison Moyet turns 65
moments.blurb: Yazoo and solo star Alison Moyet celebrates her 65th birthday on Thursday.

angles.name: That voice
angles.copy: Alison Moyet — one of the great British voices — is 65 on Thursday. Only You, Don’t Go, Love Resurrection. A proper 80s original.

moments.title: Boyz II Men’s Nathan Morris is 55
moments.blurb: Nathan Morris of Boyz II Men celebrates his birthday on Thursday.

angles.name: End of the Road
angles.copy: Boyz II Men’s Nathan Morris turns 55 on Thursday. End of the Road, I’ll Make Love to You — the 90s slow-dance soundtrack. A little Boyz II Men, coming up.

moments.title: Ladies Day at Royal Ascot
moments.blurb: Thursday is Ladies Day — the Gold Cup on the track and the boldest hats in the stands.

angles.name: Ladies Day
angles.copy: It’s Ladies Day at Ascot — the Gold Cup on the track and a fashion contest in the stands. Show us the boldest hat you can find.

angles.name: The Gold Cup
angles.copy: The Gold Cup, Ascot’s blue riband, runs today — two and a half miles and the staying test of the year. Who’s on?

moments.title: Scotland v Morocco — a place in the last 16
moments.blurb: Scotland, top of Group C after beating Haiti, face Morocco on Friday. Win and they reach the World Cup knockouts for the first time ever.

angles.name: One win from history
angles.copy: Scotland have never reached the World Cup knockouts. Beat Morocco on Friday and that changes forever. No pressure.

angles.name: Still believing
angles.copy: Top of the group, and the country’s started to believe. Scotland v Morocco, Friday. Hold your nerve, Tartan Army.

moments.title: Lionel Richie turns 77
moments.blurb: Lionel Richie celebrates his 77th birthday on Saturday.

angles.name: All Night Long
angles.copy: Lionel Richie is 77 on Saturday, and yes, we’re dancing on the ceiling all day. Easy, Hello, All Night Long — pick your favourite.

angles.name: Hello
angles.copy: Is it us you’re looking for? Lionel Richie turns 77 on Saturday. A weekend of pure smooth, coming right up.

moments.title: Duran Duran’s John Taylor is 66
moments.blurb: John Taylor, bassist of Duran Duran, celebrates his birthday on Saturday.

angles.name: Rio
angles.copy: John Taylor — the bassline behind Rio, Girls on Film and half your teenage years — is 66 on Saturday. Duran Duran all weekend.

moments.title: Royal Ascot — final day
moments.blurb: Saturday closes Royal Ascot with the Jubilee Stakes and the famous Wokingham cavalry charge.

angles.name: Last day, big bets
angles.copy: Final day at Royal Ascot — last chance to win back what the week has cost you. How’s your sweepstake looking, honestly?

moments.title: Father’s Day on the longest day
moments.blurb: Father’s Day UK falls on the summer solstice this year — Sunday 21 June.

angles.name: Immersion heater
angles.copy: Father’s Day on the longest day of the year. Maximum daylight, maximum time for him to explain how the immersion heater works.

angles.name: Dedications
angles.copy: Every dad has a song: the car one, the wedding one, the one he swears blind he saw live twice. Tell us his and we’ll play it Sunday.

angles.name: Simple and warm
angles.copy: To the dads, step-dads, grandads and the blokes who stepped up — happy Father’s Day.

moments.title: England v Ghana — matchday two
moments.blurb: England’s second group game comes on Tuesday 23rd.

angles.name: Job to do
angles.copy: Win this and England are as good as through. Lose, and we’ll do what we do best — panic for a week. Ghana, Tuesday.

moments.title: Glastonbury 2026 weekend
moments.blurb: Glastonbury returns to Worthy Farm (24–28 June); Coldplay set to headline a record-breaking fifth time.

angles.name: The tent joke
angles.copy: Right now, a quarter of a million people are pitching a tent they’ll never fold up properly again. Glastonbury’s back.

angles.name: Coldplay history
angles.copy: Coldplay headline Glastonbury for a record fifth time this weekend. Love them or not, that’s a moment.

angles.name: On our doorstep
angles.copy: For one weekend a year the population of Somerset doubles and the A37 grinds to a halt. Glastonbury’s back on our doorstep.
